cards.back: Requirement → Truth Table → Boolean Expression → Gate Circuit. Universal 3-step process.

cards.front: How many rows in a truth table with N inputs?
cards.back: 2^N rows. 3 inputs → 8 rows. 10 inputs → 1024 rows. Grows exponentially.
AND of ALL variables (complemented if 0 in row, uncomplemented if 1). Each row has a unique minterm.
Find rows where Z=1. Write minterm for each. OR them all together. Z = Σ(minterms where Z=1).
OR of ALL variables (complemented if 1 in row, uncomplemented if 0). Complement of the minterm.
Find rows where Z=0. Write maxterm for each. AND them all together. Z = Π(maxterms where Z=0).

cards.front: SOP vs POS: when to use which?
cards.back: Use SOP when fewer Z=1 rows (fewer minterms). Use POS when fewer Z=0 rows (fewer maxterms).
Three layers: NOT (inverters) → AND (one per minterm) → OR (combine all). Also called two-level logic.
Fewer gates → smaller area, lower cost, lower power, higher speed, easier to troubleshoot.
AND-OR (SOP) or OR-AND (POS) implementation. Fixed propagation delay regardless of complexity: t = t_AND + t_OR.
